to go out for an evening's entertainment
to go out for a goodtime
anyway (to get back to a previous topic)
(before an adjective or verb) to the highest degree; (the) most ...; -est
(after 之[zhi1]) best or most extreme example (e.g. 世界之最[shi4 jie4 zhi1 zui4] "the greatest in the world")
(figurative) Work too hard (with feeling of dejected)
(suffix indicating firmness, steadiness, or coming to a halt)
used after a verb to indicate a continuous state or an action which has not been completed yet
used between two verbs to indicate the two actions being performed simultaneously
used after a verb to indicate the action is temporary, similar to adding 'for now' or 'yet' after a verb
to give something to others; to have something done to oneself by others [colloquial]
as far as sth is concerned
not equal to; not as good as; inferior to
used as the and in a mixed fraction e.g. 1- ½
and (used in the context when talking about fractions)
that very (a time interval such as day
sb who passed the county level imperial exam (in Ming dynasty)
to remove or dismiss from office
to remove or dismiss from office
used in asking questions in conversations, similar to saying "is it ….?"; "Isn't it?"
for a building to have its back towards
to put something on a fire
for a building to have its back towards
to put something on a fire
(slang) 1. unkindly gossip; 2. maliciously disperse or disseminate
used as part of a rhetorical question, 'can it be that'
measure word to show the frequency of an action
to arrive at (a decision, conclusion etc)
to arrive at (a decision, conclusion etc)
measure word to show the frequency of an action
a type of top breed horse
to want; to need; to ask for
Chinese surname; (old term) unit of measuring grain
a poem used in a triad ritual
to be blindly loyal and hardworking
describes an entity which is small
noun to refer to someone with certain characteristics
usually used in a context paired with other words to address someone who is of a low or inferior status
added to the end of a term of address to express one’s affection
added to the end of something to soften the tone of something
(suffix for sb with a certain vice or addiction etc)
a term of abuse for any ignoble character,fool
(suffix) person with a certain vice or addiction etc
sly; crafty; resourceful (variant of 詭|诡[gui3])
one of the 28 constellations of ancient Chinese astronomy
also pr. [yāo] for greater clarity when spelling out numbers digit by digit
one part of a fraction, such as 'a' quarter, an eighth
a short duration (when used with a verb)
also pr. [yao1] for greater clarity when spelling out numbers digit by digit
true, original, unspoiled character of man

final particle which transforms statements into questions that indicate doubt or surprise (Cantonese)
final particle which transforms statements into questions that indicate doubt or surprise (Cantonese)
an insulting remark whilst giving someone the finger
how could this be; how can..
interjection or grunt of agreement or recognition (e.g. yes, it's me!)
